stemHtml: Which statement about VHF/UHF propagation in connection with Ground Direction Finders is correct?

options.a: VHF has, under normal conditions, a sky wave, whereas UHF has not. For both VHF/UHF propagation the space wave is the main propagation path.
options.b: Neither VHF/UHF has a sky wave under any conditions. For VHF the surface wave is the main propagation path and for UHF the space wave.
options.c: For both VHF/UHF propagation, the space wave is the only propagation path of practical use. There is no sky wave under normal conditions.
options.d: VHF-frequencies below 120 MHz may be subjected to sporadic E-reflection and then have a sky wave. For both VHF/UHF the surface wave is the only propagation path of practical use.
